title: Auditory oddball comparison (Optimum-1, Learning-oddball, and the local–global paradigm)
description: This dataset comprises raw EEG recordings from 15 participants performing three auditory oddball paradigms: Optimum-1 (optimized for mismatch negativity detection), learning-oddball (optimized for P3b), and local-global paradigm (for assessing local and global effects). The study compares within-individual sensitivity of mismatch negativity (MMN) and P3b components across these specialized oddball sequences, with data collected in a single experimental session organized into three separate recording sessions.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded from 15 participants across three auditory oddball sequences presented consecutively within a single experimental session. Most participants were recorded at 2500 Hz sampling rate (participant 13 at 5000 Hz). Each participant completed the Optimum-1 sequence for MMN assessment, learning-oddball sequence for P3b assessment, and local-global paradigm recorded in two separate EEG files (except participant 5 with three runs). Stimulation was controlled using PsychToolbox, with event timing information provided in both original trigger markers and corrected event files accounting for occasional trigger skips.
